title: caulokaempferia bracteata
intro: Caulokaempferia bracteata, also known as Thai ginger or Krachai Dam, is a type of rhizomatous plant that belongs to the Zingiberaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Thai medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Caulokaempferia bracteata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Caulokaempferia bracteata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for internal use, or applied topically in the form of oils or salves for external use.
